violations.title: Product Images Missing Alt Text
violations.description: Product images must have descriptive alt text for screen reader users.
violations.explanation: E-commerce product images are especially important for accessibility. Each product image should describe the product name, color, and key visual features so users who cannot see the image can understand what's being sold.

violations.title: Add to Cart Button Missing Name
violations.description: Shopping cart buttons must have accessible names.
violations.explanation: Buttons must have discernible text content or an aria-label attribute so screen readers can announce their purpose. Icon-only buttons like cart icons are especially prone to this issue.

violations.title: Price Text Has Low Contrast
violations.description: Sale prices and discount text do not meet contrast requirements.
violations.explanation: Text must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text. Light gray price text on white backgrounds is common in e-commerce but fails accessibility requirements.

violations.title: Search and Filter Inputs Missing Labels
violations.description: Form inputs in the product search and filter sidebar lack labels.
violations.explanation: Every form input must have a programmatically associated label. Search bars and filter inputs without labels are invisible to screen reader users.

fixSnippets.fixed:
<label for="product-search" class="sr-only">Search products</label>
<input type="search" id="product-search" placeholder="Search products...">

violations.title: Product Card Links Missing Accessible Text
violations.description: Product card links wrap images without providing accessible link text.
violations.explanation: Links must have discernible text content so users understand the link destination. When a link wraps only an image, the image's alt text becomes the link text.
